tool_args.label: NYCT and Bus Committee Meeting February 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Transit and Bus Committee Meeting February 2022. Committee Members: H. Mihaltses (Chair) V. Calise (Vice Chair) A. Albert J. Barbas N. Brown L. Cortès-Vázquez R. Glucksman D. Jones R. Linn D. Mack R. Mujica J. Samuelsen

payload.title: Transit and Bus Committee Meeting February 2022
payload.description: NYCT and Bus Committee meeting book for February 2022 meeting held on 2/22/2022 at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY. Includes executive summary, subway report, bus report, paratransit report, customer service reports, safety report, crime report, financial and ridership reports, and procurements.

2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY 10004

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: We continue to work hard alongside our partners in the city to meet several important milestones this year in our efforts to increase bus priority. Network redesign is one of our key initiatives. As an update, we are proceeding with work on the much-anticipated Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign. Starting in March, NYCDOT will begin installing new bus panels and destination signs in order to be ready for the implementation of the local bus redesign on June 26th. NYCT will also begin installing information about the redesign at bus stops in the Bronx.

payload.description: Much-anticipated Bronx local bus network redesign with NYCDOT installing new bus panels and destination signs starting March 2022, implementation on June 26, 2022. NYCT installing information about redesign at bus stops in the Bronx.

evidence_refs.source_quote: We are proceeding with work on the much-anticipated Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign. Starting in March, NYCDOT will begin installing new bus panels and destination signs in order to be ready for the implementation of the local bus redesign on June 26th.

tool_args.label: Procurement of up to 1,729 Buses (2020-2024 Capital Plan)
tool_args.raw_text: The approved 2020–2024 Capital Plan includes 2,435 buses, 706 have already been awarded. The remaining 1,729 buses include 1,042 low-floor 40-foot buses, 345 low-floor 60-foot buses, and 342 over-the-road express buses; 485 of the low-floor buses will be all-electric (460 for NYC Transit and 25 for MTABC).

payload.description: RFP authorizing resolution for purchase of up to 1,729 buses (952 NYCT + 777 MTA Bus Company), remaining in 2020-2024 Capital Plan. Includes 1,042 low-floor 40-ft, 345 low-floor 60-ft, 342 over-the-road express buses; 485 all-electric (460 NYCT, 25 MTABC). Replaces buses at end of 12-year useful life.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The approved 2020–2024 Capital Plan includes 2,435 buses, 706 have already been awarded. The remaining 1,729 buses include 1,042 low-floor 40-foot buses, 345 low-floor 60-foot buses, and 342 over-the-road express buses; 485 of the low-floor buses will be all-electric (460 for NYC Transit and 25 for MTABC).

tool_args.label: OMNY Weekly Fare Capping Pilot
tool_args.raw_text: For subway and bus customers, the primary element of MTA Best Fares is the OMNY weekly fare capping pilot which begins on Monday, February 28. With fare capping, customers will ride free for the rest of the week after taking 12 subway or local bus trips (tapping with the same card or device, Monday through Sunday).

payload.description: OMNY weekly fare capping pilot beginning February 28, 2022. Customers ride free for rest of week after 12 subway or local bus trips using same card/device Monday-Sunday. Part of MTA Best Fares program.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the OMNY weekly fare capping pilot which begins on Monday, February 28. With fare capping, customers will ride free for the rest of the week after taking 12 subway or local bus trips (tapping with the same card or device, Monday through Sunday).

tool_args.label: Transit Tech Lab Accessibility Challenge - NaviLens and Evelity Pilots
tool_args.raw_text: The two smartphone apps we are piloting through the Transit Tech Lab Accessibility Challenge are almost ready for customer testing. The NaviLens wayfinding app is being tested on the M23 SBS bus line; using colorful QR-style codes, NaviLens helps customers who are blind or low vision navigate to the exact locations of bus stops. We are additionally testing Evelity, an indoor bluetooth beacon-based wayfinding app at Jay St-MetroTech Station.

payload.description: Two smartphone app pilots through Transit Tech Lab Accessibility Challenge: NaviLens wayfinding app tested on M23 SBS bus line with QR-style codes for blind/low vision customers, and Evelity indoor bluetooth beacon-based wayfinding app at Jay St-MetroTech Station for step-by-step station navigation.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The NaviLens wayfinding app is being tested on the M23 SBS bus line... We are additionally testing Evelity, an indoor bluetooth beacon-based wayfinding app at Jay St-MetroTech Station.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: NYCT January 2022 Total Ridership
tool_args.raw_text: January 2022 New York City Transit ridership of 87.4 million was 33.6 million (27.8 percent) under budget, of which subway ridership of 63.5 million was 25.6 million (28.7 percent) below budget, and bus ridership of 23.3 million was 7.8 million (25 percent) below budget.

payload.description: January 2022 NYCT total ridership of 87.4 million was 27.8% under budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: January 2022 New York City Transit ridership of 87.4 million was 33.6 million (27.8 percent) under budget
